errors.title: Shell glob in npm test silently skips tests
errors.detail: sh has no globstar; 'node --test src/**/*.test.ts' skips nested tests. Inherited from ERR-TUNNEL-002.
errors.prevention_rule: package.json test script MUST be bare 'node --test' (recursive auto-discovery).

errors.title: Raw API key persisted
errors.detail: Raw olm_ key must exist only in the one-time approval/rotation response. Ledger stores keyId reference only; server store keeps SHA-256 hash; audit log is whitelist-projected (no key/email).
errors.prevention_rule: grep state.ts/registry.ts/audit files for 'olm_' before commit — must be zero matches; Member has keyId, never key.

errors.title: cmake target is ggml-rpc-server, not rpc-server
errors.detail: Building llama.cpp with GGML_RPC=ON: the make target is `ggml-rpc-server` (emits build/bin/rpc-server). `cmake --build -t rpc-server` fails 'No rule to make target'.
errors.prevention_rule: Build target `ggml-rpc-server llama-server`; copy build/bin/rpc-server (historic binary name).

errors.title: rpc-server default device lands on BLAS → RMS_NORM abort
errors.detail: When multiple rpc-servers share one machine, default device selection can pick BLAS, which aborts on the RMS_NORM op (proven crash) mid-completion.
errors.prevention_rule: Pass rpc-server --device explicitly (MTL0/CPU); SHARD_DEVICES overridable in cli shard up.

errors.title: Head llama-server needs -ngl 99 or the RPC devices sit idle
errors.detail: Without GPU-layer offload the model stays on the head CPU and the RPC devices hold nothing — the 'split' is fake (rpc logs never grow).
errors.prevention_rule: shardServerArgs sets -ngl 99 by default; the split proof requires BOTH rpc-server logs to grow during a completion.

errors.title: CPU-rpc reports no memory → auto-fit starves it; needs --tensor-split
errors.detail: An rpc-server on CPU reports no free memory, so llama.cpp auto-fit assigns it zero layers. One node ends up holding the whole model.
errors.prevention_rule: Pass --tensor-split (default even '1,1') so every rpc node holds layers; real ratios come from partition.ts.

errors.title: grep -q on llama-server --help causes SIGPIPE exit 141
errors.detail: Piping a long --help into `grep -q` closes the pipe early; under `set -o pipefail` the producer dies with SIGPIPE → exit 141, failing the build script.
errors.prevention_rule: Use `grep -c` (consumes all input) instead of `grep -q` when the producer is long-running.

errors.title: .local mDNS host rejected by isPrivateHost (and is a footgun)
errors.detail: isPrivateHost accepts IP literals + loopback but NOT `<host>.local`. An mDNS name can resolve off-mesh and the rpc-server binds/serves without auth (RISK-K1).
errors.prevention_rule: Members advertise the mesh IP (100.64/10), never `.local`. detectMeshHost returns the tailscale IP; .local is deliberately unsupported.

errors.title: Parallel worker's uncommitted WIP reddens the whole-repo pre-commit gate
errors.detail: The pre-commit gate runs repo-wide tsc/vitest; another session's untracked WIP (e.g. server/embed-catalog.ts + rag.ts) that doesn't compile blocks ALL commits, including unrelated green work.
errors.prevention_rule: Verify your slice is independently green (lane tsc clean, zero tsc errors from your files, your tests pass). Then GATE_SKIP=1 is justified — document it in the commit message; NEVER touch the foreign WIP.

errors.title: git add can swallow another worker's pre-staged files
errors.detail: `git add <dir>` commits whatever is ALREADY staged too. A parallel session's pre-staged files (provider-errors.ts, providers.ts) got swept into a contract commit.
errors.prevention_rule: git add file-by-file (never `git add -A`); ALWAYS `git diff --cached --name-only` before commit and unstage anything you did not author (git restore --staged).

errors.title: Member rpc unreachable over mesh despite fresh heartbeat
errors.detail: Heartbeat is outbound-only (member→operator) so a member shows 'fresh' even when the operator CANNOT reach its rpc port inbound (tailscale ACL blocks the port, or serve-rpc bound loopback not mesh). shard up --from-pool preflight then drops it.
errors.prevention_rule: Member must serve-rpc on the mesh host (not 127.0.0.1) — `contract offer` auto-detects; verify with `contract doctor` cross-host step + tailscale ACL allows 50052 between nodes.

errors.title: rpc traffic blocked by MTU / firewall on the mesh
errors.detail: llama.cpp rpc streams large tensors; an MTU mismatch or a host firewall on the rpc port stalls the head's model load (hangs, then preflight/health timeout).
errors.prevention_rule: Keep rpc on the mesh interface only (private/CGNAT); ensure the tailnet MTU is consistent; the port is never public (isPrivateHost enforces bind, RISK-K1).

errors.title: Distinct-loopback cross-host sim needs a macOS lo0 alias (sudo)
errors.detail: Simulating true cross-host (distinct HOSTS not just ports) on one macOS box needs `sudo ifconfig lo0 alias 127.0.0.2` — a one-time sudo, not a 2nd machine. Linux binds 127/8 freely.
errors.prevention_rule: Cross-host ENDPOINT path is unit-proven (buildHeadPlan/preflight over mesh IPs); the distinct-host runtime sim is opt-in (SHARD_SIM_HOSTS) — real multi-host token generation still needs a 2nd device.
